title: Expect 100-continue, Early Responses, Body Rejection, and Draining · Mastery Check
summary: A security review compares near-miss parser decisions for expect 100-continue, early responses, body rejection, and draining and requires the smallest strict expectation and early-response coordination state machine with changed-input evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for expectation and early-response coordination state machine. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Parse Expect, support 100-continue deliberately, and return appropriate final failure for unsupported expectations.
Send 100 Continue only after header-level policy accepts receiving content and before a final response commits.
Bound client waiting for 100, handle early final responses, and avoid sending content after rejection.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Expectation Grammar Policy
steps.instruction:
Order the Expectation Grammar Policy evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Expectation Grammar Policy.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Expectation Grammar Policy assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Expectation Grammar Policy, keep Expectation Grammar Policy connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

content.text: A quiz protocol team handles case 2301 in a canary edge service advertising HTTP/3 with bounded fallback. Build deterministic Go evidence to coordinate 100-continue and bounded early rejection without deadlock or unsafe reuse; preserve this operating constraint: diagnostics must omit credentials and attacker-controlled metric labels; then saturate one stream window while another remains independently serviceable. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Parse Expect, support 100-continue deliberately, and return appropriate final failure for unsupported expectations.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Expectation Grammar Policy has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.
